meta.source: termmux deep autopsy: fix all gaps, cross-platform verification, visual raster tests, prepare for public Go module
meta.maintained: Takumi must update this file after EVERY sub-task. Deviations logged within the plan.
meta.reviewGateCycle: Each task: implement → automated tests (full coverage) → ALL platform checks pass (macOS, Linux container, Windows) → lint pass (vet, staticcheck, deadcode) → Rule of Two (two contiguous PASS reviews on identical diff) → commit → update this blueprint → next task. NO EXCEPTIONS.

globalAlerts.warning: CROSS-PLATFORM MANDATE: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (macOS, Linux via `gmake make-all-in-container`, Windows via `gmake make-all-run-windows`). Run `gmake cross-build` for cross-compilation. A failure on ANY platform blocks the task.
DEADCODE MANDATE: `gmake deadcode` must pass with ZERO unreachable functions. Per AGENTS.md: never add ignores to .deadcodeignore. Wire up code or delete it.
LINT MANDATE: `gmake lint` (vet + staticcheck + deadcode) must pass after EVERY code change.
TDD DISCIPLINE: Write failing test FIRST, then implement the fix. A test that doesn't fail before the fix is not a valid test.
REVIEW GATE: Rule of Two — two contiguous PASS reviews from separate subagents on the EXACT SAME diff before ANY commit. If code changes after pass 1, the counter resets to 0.
MODULE ISOLATION: The termmux package must have zero imports from OSM-internal packages outside termmux/. It must be extractable as a standalone Go module.
NO PRODUCT BRANDING: The termmux package must contain zero product-specific strings (no '[Claude]'). All branding is set externally via SetTitle or factory functions.
STRICT REVIEW GATE CYCLE: Each task follows: implement → automated tests → ALL platform checks → lint → Rule of Two → commit → blueprint update → next task. NO SHORTCUTS.

mandatoryDirectives.contextRefresh: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start. Resume from first non-Done task.
mandatoryDirectives.incrementalUpdate: Update task status after EVERY sub-task. Never batch updates.
mandatoryDirectives.noEstimates: Perfection isn't cheap — no effort estimates allowed.
mandatoryDirectives.noPriorities: All tasks are mandatory — no prioritization.
mandatoryDirectives.tddDiscipline: Every fix requires a failing test first. Tests that never fail are not tests.
mandatoryDirectives.crossPlatform: After every code change, verify: (1) local build+test, (2) gmake lint, (3) gmake deadcode. Before marking Done: (4) gmake cross-build, (5) Linux container test, (6) Windows test.

statusSection.currentState: Replanning. All GAP/KILL fixes implemented locally. Windows test failures from stale [Claude] assertions fixed. deadcode clean after wiring RestoreFromState and RenderRaster to JS bindings. Cross-platform verification in progress.
statusSection.goalState: The termmux package is defect-free, fully tested with visual raster assertions, all autopsy findings verified TRUE/FALSE/CANNOT VERIFY (zero UNCERTAIN), deadcode clean on all platforms, lint clean on all platforms, tests pass on macOS + Linux + Windows, and the package is ready for extraction as a public open-source Go module.

replanLog.trigger: Session replan — Hana-san mandated blueprint replan with strict review gate cycle enforcement per task, cross-platform verification, and proper status tracking
replanLog.summary: Old blueprint had stale 'Not Started' status on completed tasks, no cross-platform verification steps, no per-task review gate enforcement. Rewriting entire sequentialTasks with accurate status, explicit cross-platform checks per task, and the strict review gate cycle as part of each task's acceptance criteria.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix stale [Claude] assertions in passthrough_test.go causing Windows failures
sequentialTasks.description: Two tests in `internal/termmux/passthrough_test.go` still assert that stdout contains '[Claude]': (1) `TestPassthroughStatusBar_ScrollRegionSetup` line 497 — change from `!strings.Contains(got, "[Claude]")` to assert 'switch' toggle hint appears and '[Claude]' does NOT appear; (2) `TestPassthroughStatusBar_RenderRestore` lines 597-601 — change from counting '[Claude]' occurrences to counting 'switch' toggle hint occurrences. These tests were not updated when GAP-002 was fixed (StatusBar title made configurable with no default branding).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both tests pass on macOS (local), Linux (container), and Windows (gmake make-all-run-windows). No test asserts '[Claude]' appears in termmux output. The statusbar package tests already verify '[Claude]' does NOT appear by default. `gmake deadcode` and `gmake lint` pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire RestoreFromState and RenderRaster to JS bindings for deadcode compliance
sequentialTasks.description: The `deadcode` tool reported `SessionManager.RestoreFromState` (persistence.go:126) and all `vt/render_raster.go` functions as unreachable. Per AGENTS.md: never add .deadcodeignore entries — wire up the code. Added JS bindings in `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`: (1) `restoreState(state)` binding that converts JS state object back to `PersistedManagerState` and calls `mgr.RestoreFromState` with a default factory creating `CaptureSession` from persisted config; (2) `renderRaster(id, options?)` binding that takes a snapshot, reconstructs a VTerm screen, calls `vt.RenderRaster`, saves to PNG temp file, returns dimensions and path. Also call `vt.RenderRasterDefault` when default cell size is used to ensure it's reachable.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `gmake deadcode` reports zero unreachable functions. The JS bindings compile and the `builtin/termmux` package tests pass. `go test -short ./internal/builtin/termmux/...` passes. Cross-platform build (`gmake cross-build`) passes. `gmake lint` passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Cross-platform verification: ensure ALL termmux tests pass on macOS, Linux, Windows
sequentialTasks.description: Run the full verification gauntlet: (1) `go test -short -race ./internal/termmux/...` on macOS (local), (2) `gmake cross-build` for Linux/macOS/Windows cross-compilation, (3) `docker run golang:X go test -short ./internal/termmux/...` for Linux container, (4) `gmake make-all-run-windows` for Windows (build+test+lint). Record all results in WIP.md. Fix any platform-specific failures found.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All termmux tests pass on macOS, Linux container, and Windows. `gmake cross-build` succeeds for all 4 targets (linux/amd64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64). `gmake lint` passes. `gmake deadcode` passes. WIP.md updated with cross-platform results for each platform.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two review on current termmux diff (all GAP/KILL fixes + JS bindings + test fixes)
sequentialTasks.description: Spawn two separate review subagents (Pass 1, then Pass 2) on `git diff HEAD -- internal/termmux/ internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`. Each subagent must verify: (1) All Process.Write callers use []byte signature, (2) filterMouseForStatusBar 3-return-value signature used consistently, (3) forwardStdin carry-over deep-copy prevents aliasing, (4) CaptureSession.Write propagates n from Process.Write, (5) RestoreFromState validates nil state/factory/wrong version, (6) No [Claude] branding in termmux package, (7) deadcode clean, (8) All passthrough tests updated for configurable title. If any review FAILS, fix issues and restart from Pass 1.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Two contiguous PASS reviews exist on the exact same diff. Review artifacts saved to `./scratch/termmux-review-pass-1.md` and `./scratch/termmux-review-pass-2.md`. Both reviews confirm no blocking findings.

sequentialTasks.task: Commit termmux GAP/KILL fixes with Kubernetes-style commit message
sequentialTasks.description: After Rule of Two passes, commit all termmux changes with a single descriptive commit message. The commit must include: GAP-001 (RestoreFromState), GAP-002 (StatusBar configurable title), GAP-003 (forwardStdin extraction), GAP-004 (Dirty field removal), GAP-005/KILL-001 (sync.Once idempotent Start), GAP-007/018 (Process.Write io.Writer), GAP-008 (Rows/Cols accessors), GAP-009 (slices.SortFunc), GAP-010 (writeOrLog Warn), GAP-014/020 (PassthroughConfig relocation + NoTerminal), GAP-016 (BufferedWriter doc), GAP-019 (BufferedReader copy elimination), KILL-006 (SGR mouse carry-over buffer), JS bindings (restoreState, renderRaster), test fixes ([Claude] assertions). After committing, verify `gmake` (full build+test+lint) passes on the committed state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A single commit exists with all termmux changes. `gmake` passes on macOS. `git diff HEAD` shows no uncommitted changes in internal/termmux/. The commit message follows Kubernetes-style format.

sequentialTasks.task: Create raster test harness in internal/termmux/rastertest/ package
sequentialTasks.description: Create `internal/termmux/rastertest/` with a reusable test harness: (1) `SpawnCapture(ctx, cfg CaptureConfig) (*CaptureSession, func())` — spawns a PTY process and returns a CaptureSession + cleanup, (2) `FeedOutput(vterm *vt.VTerm, cs *CaptureSession, timeout time.Duration) error` — reads CaptureSession output and feeds it into a VTerm until timeout or EOF, (3) `RenderAndAssert(t, vterm *vt.VTerm, cellW, cellH int) *image.RGBA` — renders VTerm to raster, (4) `AssertCellFG(t, img *image.RGBA, row, col, cellW, cellH int, want color.RGBA)` — asserts a cell's foreground color, (5) `AssertCellBG(t, img *image.RGBA, row, col, cellW, cellH int, want color.RGBA)` — asserts a cell's background color, (6) `skipIfNoPTY(t)` — skips on platforms without PTY support. The package must compile on all platforms (use build constraints for PTY-dependent code).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The rastertest package compiles and exports the harness functions. A test `TestHarness_SpawnEcho` spawns `echo hello`, captures output through VTerm, renders a raster, and asserts the image has correct dimensions. Test passes on macOS with a real PTY, skips gracefully on platforms without PTY. `gmake lint` and `gmake deadcode` pass. `gmake cross-build` passes. Cross-platform verification run.

sequentialTasks.task: Visual raster test: basic text output through PTY → VTerm → raster
sequentialTasks.description: Using the rastertest harness, add `TestRaster_BasicText` that: (1) Spawns `echo hello` in a PTY via `rastertest.SpawnCapture`, (2) Feeds output through VTerm via `rastertest.FeedOutput`, (3) Renders to raster via `vt.RenderRaster`, (4) Asserts the first row contains non-zero pixels in the first 5 cell positions (for 'hello'). Also add `TestRaster_ANSIColor` that spawns a command producing colored output (e.g., `printf '\033[31mred\033[0m'`) and verifies the foreground color of the 'red' cells is approximately red (R > 200, G < 50, B < 50). Also add `TestRaster_Scrolling` that produces more lines than the terminal height and verifies scrolled content is correct. All tests use `testing.Short()` skip guards.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All three visual raster tests pass on macOS (Unix PTY). Images are saved to test output directory for manual inspection. `gmake lint` and `gmake deadcode` pass. `go test -short` skips these tests. `go test` (full) runs them. Cross-platform verification: Linux container passes, Windows skips gracefully.

sequentialTasks.task: Visual raster test: resize and wide character handling
sequentialTasks.description: Add `TestRaster_Resize` that: (1) Spawns a long-running process (e.g., `cat`), (2) Writes text, (3) Resizes the PTY from 80x24 to 40x24, (4) Writes more text, (5) Captures VTerm state, (6) Renders to raster, (7) Verifies raster width is 40*cellW pixels. Add `TestRaster_WideChars` that produces CJK characters (e.g., `echo '你好'`) and verifies they occupy 2 cells in the raster (double-width cells have non-zero pixels spanning twice the normal cell width). Both tests use rastertest harness with `testing.Short()` skip guards.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both visual raster tests pass on macOS. Resize test verifies raster dimensions reflect new terminal size. Wide char test verifies double-width rendering. `gmake lint` and `gmake deadcode` pass. Cross-platform verification.

sequentialTasks.task: Visual raster test: StatusBar rendering without product branding
sequentialTasks.description: Add `TestRaster_StatusBar_Generic` that: (1) Creates a VTerm with 24 rows, (2) Sets up a StatusBar with default (no) title on the last row, (3) Fills all rows with content, (4) Renders the VTerm + StatusBar to raster, (5) Verifies the last row contains the status bar text (toggle key hint like 'switch') and does NOT contain '[Claude]'. This test explicitly verifies GAP-002 fix — no product branding in the raster output. Also test with `SetTitle('myapp')` to verify custom title appears as '[myapp]' in the raster.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test passes and explicitly asserts '[Claude]' does NOT appear in the status bar row. Custom title '[myapp]' DOES appear when SetTitle is used. `gmake lint` and `gmake deadcode` pass. Cross-platform verification.

sequentialTasks.task: Rewrite autopsy 06_honest_conclusions.md: resolve all UNCERTAIN findings
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite `scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/06_honest_conclusions.md`. Every 'UNCERTAIN' finding must be resolved with test evidence: (1) 'Performance under high session counts' — run benchmark with 50+ sessions, classify as TRUE or FALSE with data. (2) 'TestSpawn_EchoHello flake' — root-cause timing dependency, classify. (3) 'SGR mouse filtering at buffer boundaries' — now verified by KILL-006 fix and carry-over tests, classify as FALSE (was broken, now fixed). (4) 'Dirty field was intended for incremental rendering' — verified by removal and passing tests, classify as TRUE (dead code, never consumed). (5) 'Persistence was designed for round-trip' — verified by RestoreFromState implementation, classify as TRUE (intent confirmed, feature now complete).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 5 previously-UNCERTAIN findings are resolved. The document contains ZERO 'UNCERTAIN' entries. Every finding is TRUE (with code evidence), FALSE (with test evidence), or CANNOT VERIFY (with explicit evidence gap explanation).

sequentialTasks.task: Rewrite autopsy 04_critical_failures.md: update KILL conditions with verification results
sequentialTasks.description: Update `scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/04_critical_failures.md`: KILL-001 FIXED (sync.Once idempotent Start, test proves no panic), KILL-006 FIXED (SGR mouse carry-over buffer, test proves partial sequences are buffered), KILL-002/003/004 CONFIRMED HANDLED (with specific test references), KILL-005 ACCEPTABLE RISK (with note about panic recovery). Add summary table with status for each KILL condition.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Every KILL condition has verified status: FIXED (with test reference), CONFIRMED HANDLED (with test reference), or ACCEPTABLE RISK (with justification). No unresolved KILL conditions.

sequentialTasks.task: Rewrite autopsy 03_gap_analysis.md: update all gaps with fix status
sequentialTasks.description: Update `scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/03_gap_analysis.md` to reflect all fixes: GAP-001 FIXED (RestoreFromState), GAP-002 FIXED (configurable title), GAP-003 FIXED (forwardStdin extraction), GAP-004 FIXED (Dirty removed), GAP-005 FIXED (sync.Once), GAP-007/018 FIXED (io.Writer), GAP-008 FIXED (Rows/Cols), GAP-009 FIXED (slices.SortFunc), GAP-010 FIXED (Warn), GAP-011 VERIFIED (already existed), GAP-012 FIXED (makeLine removed with Dirty), GAP-013 SKIPPED (cosmetic, package prefixes acceptable), GAP-014 FIXED (PassthroughConfig relocated), GAP-016 FIXED (doc corrected), GAP-017 CANNOT FIX (uniseg v0.4.7 lacks RuneWidth), GAP-019 FIXED (copy eliminated), GAP-020 FIXED (NoTerminal sentinel). Each entry includes test reference and code change location.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Every gap has final status: FIXED (with test reference and file location), CANNOT FIX (with explicit justification), or RECLASSIFIED (with reasoning). No gaps left in original state.

sequentialTasks.task: Rewrite autopsy 05_evidence.md: add all test results, benchmarks, and raster images
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite `scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/05_evidence.md` with complete empirical data: (1) Full test suite results (all passing), (2) Cross-platform results (macOS, Linux, Windows), (3) Lint results (vet, staticcheck, deadcode — all clean), (4) Race detector results, (5) Coverage data from `go test -cover ./internal/termmux/...`, (6) Visual raster test results with PNG file paths and descriptions, (7) Rule of Two review results.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The evidence document contains concrete, reproducible test results for every finding. No 'uncertain' or 'untested' areas. All claims backed by specific test names and output.

sequentialTasks.task: Rewrite autopsy README.md: updated verdict reflecting all fixes
sequentialTasks.description: Rewrite `scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/README.md` with new verdict. Original highlighted three defect categories. New verdict reflects: all HIGH/CRITICAL gaps fixed, all KILL conditions resolved, visual raster tests verify terminal behavior end-to-end, cross-platform verification complete, package ready for public module extraction. Update reading order, document index, and caveats.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: README reflects current codebase state after all fixes. Verdict is honest and evidence-based. Document index updated.

sequentialTasks.task: Public module readiness audit: verify zero OSM-internal imports, export review, go.mod
sequentialTasks.description: Run `go list -deps ./internal/termmux/...` and verify all dependencies are external (uniseg, creack/pty, x/term, x/sys). Search termmux package for any remaining product-specific strings. Review all exported types/methods for Go naming conventions (no prepositions per AGENTS.md). Verify all doc comments are accurate (no fabricated features). Create proposed `go.mod` for standalone module (termmux v0.1.0).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Module isolation verified — all deps are external. Zero product-specific strings. All exported APIs follow Go naming conventions. All doc comments accurate. Package is extractable as standalone module with no modifications beyond go.mod/go.sum.

sequentialTasks.task: Full verification gauntlet: lint + test + race + coverage on all platforms
sequentialTasks.description: Run complete verification: (1) `gmake lint` locally, (2) `go test -race ./internal/termmux/...` locally, (3) `go test -cover ./internal/termmux/...` and verify >= 80% coverage per sub-package, (4) `gmake cross-build` for all 4 targets, (5) Linux container: `docker run golang:X go test -short -race ./internal/termmux/...`, (6) Windows: `gmake make-all-run-windows`. Record all results in WIP.md. Fix any issues found.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All checks pass on ALL platforms. Coverage >= 80% for every sub-package. No race conditions. No vet/staticcheck/deadcode warnings. WIP.md documents all results with platform-specific data.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two final review on complete termmux diff (all changes from HEAD)
sequentialTasks.description: After all tasks above are complete, run a final Rule of Two review on the complete diff: `git diff HEAD -- internal/termmux/ internal/builtin/termmux/module.go scratch/termmux-autopsy-20260502/`. This is a holistic review of ALL changes. Both subagents must PASS on the exact same diff. If any review fails, fix and restart from Pass 1.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Two contiguous PASS reviews on the complete diff. Review artifacts at `./scratch/termmux-final-review-pass-1.md` and `./scratch/termmux-final-review-pass-2.md`. Both confirm no blocking findings.

sequentialTasks.task: Final commit: all termmux changes including raster tests and autopsy rewrites
sequentialTasks.description: After final Rule of Two passes, commit ALL remaining termmux changes: rastertest package, visual raster tests, autopsy document rewrites, module audit results. Verify `gmake` passes on the committed state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A single commit (or series of logical commits) with all remaining changes. `gmake` passes on macOS. `gmake make-all-in-container` passes on Linux. `gmake make-all-run-windows` passes on Windows. `git status` shows clean working tree.

sequentialTasks.task: Scope expansion: identify next frontier for True Perfection
sequentialTasks.description: After all current tasks are Done, ideate and add new improvements: (1) Incremental VTerm rendering using dirty tracking (re-implement with actual consumer), (2) PTY process reattach support (live PID reconnection in RestoreFromState), (3) Benchmark-driven optimization of VTerm write path, (4) Fuzz testing for VTerm parser against malformed sequences, (5) Terminal multiplexer TUI demo binary, (6) API documentation for standalone module. Add these as new tasks in the blueprint.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: New tasks added to sequentialTasks in this blueprint. Each meets the Planning Depth Standard with specific files, acceptance criteria, and context pointers. The task list is never empty.

crossPlatformCommands: docker run --rm -v $(pwd):/work -w /work golang:$(go mod edit -print | awk '/^go /{print $2}') bash -lc 'go test -short -count=1 ./internal/termmux/...'
continuousVerification.coverageRequirement: All termmux sub-packages must have >= 80% test coverage. Verify with go test -cover ./internal/termmux/...
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails on ANY platform, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: CROSS-PLATFORM: ALL checks must pass on macOS, Linux, AND Windows. No exceptions.
DEADCODE: gmake deadcode must report zero unreachable functions. Wire up or delete — never .deadcodeignore.
REVIEW GATE: Rule of Two — two contiguous PASS reviews on identical diff before any commit. Counter resets on code change.
MODULE ISOLATION: termmux must be extractable as standalone Go module. Zero OSM-internal imports.
NO PRODUCT BRANDING: No '[Claude]' or any product-specific strings in the termmux package.
